terms.definition: Prescribed nutrient-dense drinks, puddings or powders used to supplement intake when food alone is insufficient.
terms.exampleSentence: Two oral nutritional supplements per day were prescribed alongside food fortification advice.
terms.contextNotes: Take between, not with, meals to avoid suppressing appetite.

terms.definition: Enteral nutrition delivered via a fine-bore tube passed through the nose into the stomach for short-term feeding.
terms.exampleSentence: Nasogastric feeding was commenced to meet her energy needs while her swallow recovered.
terms.contextNotes: Confirm position by pH and CXR per local policy.

terms.definition: Enteral feeding via a tube passed through the nose into the jejunum, used when gastric feeding is contraindicated or not tolerated.
terms.exampleSentence: Nasojejunal feeding was selected because of recurrent gastric reflux and aspiration risk.
terms.contextNotes: Run continuously; bolus feeding usually unsuitable.

terms.definition: Enteral feeding via a percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy tube placed through the abdominal wall into the stomach.
terms.exampleSentence: PEG feeding was planned to provide longer-term nutrition support after her stroke.
terms.contextNotes: Used when enteral feeding likely beyond four weeks.

terms.definition: Enteral feeding via a radiologically inserted gastrostomy tube, used when endoscopic insertion is unsuitable.
terms.exampleSentence: RIG feeding was offered as PEG insertion was contraindicated by his head and neck cancer.
terms.contextNotes: Common in head and neck cancer pathways.

terms.definition: Intravenous administration of energy, protein, fluid, electrolytes and micronutrients when the gut cannot be used safely.
terms.exampleSentence: Parenteral nutrition was commenced via central line as the bowel had failed.
terms.contextNotes: MDT decision; monitor for line sepsis and refeeding.

terms.definition: Strategy of adding energy- or protein-dense foods to usual meals to increase nutritional value without large volumes.
terms.exampleSentence: Food fortification advice was provided, including adding cream and grated cheese to meals.
terms.contextNotes: First-line for mild to moderate undernutrition.

terms.definition: Diet in which food is altered in texture and fluids in thickness to enable safer swallowing in dysphagia.
terms.exampleSentence: A modified texture diet at IDDSI level 5 was advised to reduce her aspiration risk.
terms.contextNotes: Always document IDDSI level for food and fluids.

terms.definition: International Dysphagia Diet Standardisation Initiative framework defining 0–7 levels of food and fluid texture.
terms.exampleSentence: Her plan specifies IDDSI level 4 fluids and level 6 food textures.
terms.contextNotes: Use the standardised IDDSI testing methods at point of service.

terms.definition: Prescribed daily upper limit on total fluid intake to manage conditions such as heart failure or hyponatraemia.
terms.exampleSentence: A 1.5 litre fluid restriction was set in view of his heart failure and oedema.
terms.contextNotes: Account for medications, IVs and food moisture.

terms.definition: Short-term diet low in fibre and other residue used to reduce stool bulk, often before bowel surgery or in flares.
terms.exampleSentence: A low-residue diet was advised in the lead-up to his elective bowel surgery.
terms.contextNotes: Not for long-term use; reintroduce fibre when able.

terms.definition: Very low carbohydrate, high fat dietary regimen designed to induce ketosis for therapeutic purposes such as drug-resistant epilepsy.
terms.exampleSentence: The ketogenic diet was initiated under specialist supervision for her drug-resistant epilepsy.
terms.contextNotes: Specialist initiation only; monitor growth and lipids.

terms.definition: Dietary Approaches to Stop Hypertension eating pattern emphasising fruit, vegetables, whole grains, low-fat dairy and reduced sodium.
terms.exampleSentence: The DASH diet was recommended to support her blood pressure management.
terms.contextNotes: Pair with sodium reduction and physical activity.

terms.definition: Eating pattern rich in olive oil, vegetables, legumes, fish and whole grains, with moderate dairy and limited red meat.
terms.exampleSentence: A Mediterranean diet was discussed for cardiovascular risk reduction.
terms.contextNotes: Strong evidence for cardiovascular benefit.

terms.definition: Person-centred counselling style that strengthens a patient's own motivation and commitment to behaviour change.
terms.exampleSentence: Motivational interviewing was used to explore her ambivalence about reducing alcohol intake.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid the righting reflex; use OARS skills.

terms.definition: Goal-setting framework where objectives are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant and Time-bound.
terms.exampleSentence: We agreed two SMART goals to review at her next dietetic appointment in four weeks.
terms.contextNotes: Co-create goals with the patient; document clearly.

terms.definition: Process of modifying habits using evidence-based techniques such as goal setting, self-monitoring and problem-solving.
terms.exampleSentence: Behaviour change techniques including self-monitoring were introduced during the consultation.
terms.contextNotes: Match technique to readiness to change.

terms.definition: UK front-of-pack labelling using red, amber and green to indicate high, medium or low fat, saturated fat, sugars and salt.
terms.exampleSentence: We reviewed traffic light food labelling so she could choose lower-salt options.
terms.contextNotes: Aim for mainly green and amber; limit red items.

terms.definition: UK dietary reference value estimating the daily intake sufficient to meet the needs of nearly all healthy individuals.
terms.exampleSentence: Her calcium intake was below the reference nutrient intake for her age group.
terms.contextNotes: EAR estimates average needs; RNI is higher for population coverage.
commonMistakes: applying to individuals as a target

terms.definition: Dietary reference value reflecting the average daily nutrient need of a population group.
terms.exampleSentence: Her usual intake was just below the estimated average requirement for iron.
terms.contextNotes: Useful for assessing groups, not individuals.

terms.definition: Structured clinical record of dietetic assessment, diagnosis, intervention, monitoring and evaluation following the ADIME format.
terms.exampleSentence: A dietetic consultation note using the ADIME format was filed in her electronic record.
terms.contextNotes: Include PES statement: problem, etiology, signs/symptoms.

terms.definition: Identification of a specific nutrition problem that the dietitian is responsible for treating, written as a PES statement.
terms.exampleSentence: The nutrition diagnosis was inadequate oral intake related to reduced appetite, evidenced by 6% weight loss.
terms.contextNotes: Should be measurable and resolvable through dietetic action.

terms.definition: Ongoing measurement of agreed indicators to determine progress and outcomes of dietetic intervention.
terms.exampleSentence: Monitoring and evaluation will include weekly weights and a food diary review.
terms.contextNotes: Tie indicators directly to the nutrition diagnosis.

terms.definition: Collaborative process where clinicians and patients agree on a plan based on evidence and personal values and preferences.
terms.exampleSentence: Shared decision making guided the choice between PEG insertion and continued NG feeding.
terms.contextNotes: Use plain language and check understanding.

terms.definition: Coordinated process to ensure safe transition of nutrition care from one setting to another, including community follow-up.
terms.exampleSentence: Discharge planning included home enteral feeding training and a community dietitian referral.
terms.contextNotes: Confirm equipment, supplies and follow-up before discharge.
